title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 414.112 - Deception of customers in sale of fuels as to nature, quality or identity, prohibited — reclaimed motor or lubricating oils — containers, how marked — interference with director, prohibited
text:
414.112.  Deception of customers in sale of fuels as to nature, quality or identity, prohibited — reclaimed motor or lubricating oils — containers, how marked — interference with director, prohibited. — 1.  No person shall store, sell, expose for sale, or offer for sale, gasoline, diesel fuel, heating oil, kerosene, aviation turbine fuel, gasoline-alcohol blends or other motor fuels, so as to deceive or tend to deceive the purchaser as to the nature, quality, and identity of the product so sold or offered for sale, or under any name whatsoever except the true trade name thereof.
  2.  No person shall store, sell, expose for sale, or offer for sale, any reclaimed motor or lubricating oils; except that all drums, cans, or other containers, holding such reclaimed motor or lubricating oils before being offered for sale, shall have imprinted thereon, in contrasting color with the other surface of the container, in letters not less than one-half inch in height, wording specifying "reclaimed" motor or lubricating oil.
  3.  No person shall hinder or obstruct the director, or his delegated representative, in the reasonable performance of his duties.
(L. 1987 S.B. 249) 
Effective 1-01-88
